title: scirpus microcarpus subsp. rubrotinctus
intro: Scirpus microcarpus subsp. rubrotinctus, also known as the dwarf bulrush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Scirpus microcarpus subsp. rubrotinctus include the potential treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
